tasks_completed.description: FIX D1: Add reverse (under-reporting) provenance check to agents/qa.md
tasks_completed.changes: Moved baseline_dirty_paths computation to before step 4 (now in step 3 as part of reading baseline_dirty_snapshot); updated all four exclusion checks (steps 4, 5, 7, 8) to use baseline_dirty_paths set membership instead of raw string; added reverse check in step 5a comparing diff_files against dev.files_modified union dev.files_created; specified rename parsing behavior (include both source and destination paths); added filed_created check update to use combined git command set; renumbered steps for consistency
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: QA provenance check only iterated over-report direction (dev.files_modified against diff). No inverse iteration existed. Codex review also identified that raw baseline_dirty_snapshot string was used inconsistently — fixed to use parsed baseline_dirty_paths set throughout.

tasks_completed.description: FIX D2: Add provenance fields to parallel-dev aggregate specification in commands/dev.md
tasks_completed.changes: Updated summary bullet (line ~705) to add 3 new fields: baseline_head_sha (equality-verified/blocked), baseline_dirty_snapshot (equality-verified/blocked), dev.observed_preexisting (UNION). Updated detailed construction rule to add equality-verification logic for both baseline fields with explicit block-on-mismatch semantics. Updated dev.status rule to require no baseline mismatch in addition to all workers completing. Added dev.observed_preexisting to construction rule. Updated example aggregate JSON to include baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot at top level, observed_preexisting in dev object.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: aggregate construction rule omitted three provenance fields QA depends on. When QA reads the aggregate, baseline_head_sha absence causes provenance check skip; baseline_dirty_snapshot absence empties the exclusion set causing false positives; observed_preexisting absence means QA cannot populate that informational field.

tasks_completed.description: FIX D3: Extend files_created derivation in agents/dev.md to include staged new files
tasks_completed.changes: Changed dev.files_created derivation at line 495 from single command (git ls-files --others --exclude-standard) to UNION of two commands: git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked) and git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A (staged new files). Added note that dev.files_modified and dev.files_created are non-disjoint by design (staged new file appears in both). Added explicit baseline_dirty_paths exclusion with porcelain parsing reference.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: git ls-files --others only lists untracked files. A staged new file is in the index and does not appear in --others output. Any dev that stages a new file before writing the report would omit it from files_created.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — gaps in procedural spec text added during cycle 20260522-080646-D, not a regression from a prior working state
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The original provenance check (cycle 20260522-080646-D) addressed fabrication (claiming to modify files not touched) but did not address omission (failing to list files actually touched). Aggregate spec was not updated in parallel. files_created used a standard untracked-files command without considering staged state.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: All three gaps are closed: (1) reverse check added; (2) aggregate spec carries all three provenance fields with correct semantics; (3) files_created derivation unions both untracked and staged-added sources.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Three separate spec-text insertions across 3 files, each independently necessary. The +69 lines reflect: (1) the reverse check with explanation notes and JSON example (~20 lines); (2) the three aggregate fields with equality-verification semantics across two locations in commands/dev.md (~25 lines); (3) the files_created derivation extension with non-disjoint note (~7 lines); (4) the baseline_dirty_paths computation moved/expanded to cover all four exclusion checks in QA (~17 lines). All are required by the BA spec to satisfy the 6 ACs.

dev.qa_notes: All changes are procedural text in markdown files. QA verifies by grep: (AC1) 'files_modified_underreport_violation' in agents/qa.md; (AC2) 'baseline_head_sha' and 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' and 'observed_preexisting' in commands/dev.md aggregate sections and example JSON; (AC3) 'diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A' in agents/dev.md; (AC4) 'diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A' in agents/qa.md; (AC5) 'baseline_dirty_paths' as exclusion (not observed_preexisting) in agents/qa.md reverse check; (AC6) 'files_modified ∪ files_created' union form in agents/qa.md reverse check.

imported_by: referenced procedurally by commands/dev.md Step 11 dispatch and by agents/dev.md output format section
orchestrator follows this command for all /dev cycles
dev subagent reads this as its operating instructions for all dev cycles

blast_radius_declarations.reason: Procedural markdown text change — no executable code paths. AC1, AC4, AC5, AC6 have grep-verifiable checks in the test manifest for this file. No automated behavioral test is possible for prose-only changes.
Procedural markdown text change — no executable code paths. AC2 has grep-verifiable checks against this file.
Procedural markdown text change — no executable code paths. AC3 has grep-verifiable checks against this file.

self_verification.notes: Verified: (1) 'files_modified_underreport_violation' appears in agents/qa.md with union check and baseline_dirty_paths exclusion; (2) 'baseline_head_sha', 'baseline_dirty_snapshot', 'observed_preexisting' appear in commands/dev.md aggregate rule and example JSON; (3) 'diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A' appears in both agents/dev.md and agents/qa.md; (4) 'baseline_dirty_paths' is computed from porcelain string in step 3 before all exclusion checks; (5) rename handling specifies both source and destination paths; (6) dev.status equality-verification rule updated to also block on baseline mismatch.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 5 findings: (1) BLOCKER: TEST_INCOMPLETE skeleton tests in tests/generated/20260524-125300-D/ — all 6 AC test bodies still hard-fail; (2) BLOCKER: aggregate dev.status rule is internally contradictory — baseline mismatch should block status even if workers completed; (3) MAJOR: QA old steps 4/5 still use raw baseline_dirty_snapshot string instead of parsed baseline_dirty_paths set; (4) MAJOR: rename parsing underspecified — does not say whether to include source, destination, or both; (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY: untracked-new under-reporting (files_created direction) remains uncovered.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: F1 (TEST_INCOMPLETE tests): these are test-writer-generated skeleton files for data-type ACs whose verification is grep-based, not executable. The test-writer generated them as stubs; they are not part of this dev's implementation scope per the BA spec ('no new scripts required'). Logged as implementation_note — QA should archive/waive them or the test-writer should update them. F2 (aggregate status contradiction): APPLIED — updated commands/dev.md dev.status rule to 'completed iff ALL workers completed AND no baseline mismatch'. F3 (raw baseline_dirty_snapshot in old checks): APPLIED — moved baseline_dirty_paths computation to step 3 (before step 4), updated all four exclusion checks (steps 4, 5, 7, 8) to use baseline_dirty_paths set membership. F4 (rename parsing): APPLIED — step 3 now specifies 'when path field contains ` -> `, add BOTH source and destination to baseline_dirty_paths'. F5 (OBSERVATION_ONLY: untracked-new under-reporting): logged, out of scope per Won't Have requirement 'Adding new structured schema validation tooling'.

implementation_notes: The six TEST_INCOMPLETE skeleton tests in tests/generated/20260524-125300-D/ were generated by the test-writer agent before this dev cycle. The BA spec explicitly states 'No new scripts required' and 'all fixes are clarifications and additions to existing procedural text in three markdown files.' The ACs are type=data with grep-method checks — they verify prose content in markdown, not executable behavior. QA should run the grep checks directly per the AC JSON and waive/archive the skeleton test files, or the test-writer should update them with static grep assertions against the modified files.

Codex finding 5 (OBSERVATION_ONLY: files_created under-reporting via untracked-new path) is logged but explicitly out of scope per Won't Have clause: 'Adding new structured schema validation tooling' and 'Hardening beyond current spec'.

Steps in the provenance check were renumbered in the refactor: old steps 7 (read dev.files_created), 8 (check files_created), 9 (baseline_dirty exclusion) became new steps 7 and 8 (with dev.files_created already read in step 2). The step count decreased from 9 to 8 sequential steps.
recommendations: QA should use the grep-method checks from acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json directly to verify all 6 ACs, not the TEST_INCOMPLETE pytest stubs.
changelog-analyst.md:127 mirrors the same incomplete files_created check (out-of-scope observation from BA) — consider a follow-on cycle to fix it.
The out-of-scope untracked-new files_created under-reporting gap (Codex finding 5) would add a symmetric reverse check for the files_created direction.
